name: Install SimSystem on Windows via mpm
description: Tests the full Windows workflow: confirmation, plan display, PowerShell safety gate, input file preparation, and install command generation
prompt: I need to install SimSystem for R2025a on my Windows machine. Put it in C:\MatrixEngine\R2025a. My username is testuser.
expected_output: Agent confirms selections, displays installation plan, writes .ps1 scripts with Write tool, downloads mpm from correct URL, and never uses powershell.exe -Command

assertions.description: Agent confirms release, destination, and products before executing
assertions.expected: A confirmation message listing R2025a, C:\MatrixEngine\R2025a, and SimSystem before any commands run

assertions.description: Agent displays an installation plan after confirmation and before execution
assertions.expected: A plan showing numbered steps: download mpm, prepare input file, run installation, verify

assertions.description: Agent writes PowerShell scripts via the Write file tool, never uses powershell.exe -Command
assertions.expected: All .ps1 files created with Write tool; invoked with powershell.exe ... -File <path>

assertions.description: Agent uses the correct mpm download URL for Windows
Agent never uses powershell.exe -Command inline

name: Install MatrixEngine and Signal Processing Toolbox on Linux
description: Tests Linux workflow: correct mpm URL, input file from mathworks.com, product lookup, and verification artifact
prompt: Install MatrixEngine and Signal Processing Toolbox for 25b on my Linux box. Install to /usr/local/MatrixEngine/R2025b.
expected_output: Agent expands '25b' to 'R2025b', downloads input file from mathworks.com, uses correct product identifiers from template, uses Linux mpm URL, and verifies completion artifact

assertions.description: Agent expands shorthand release '25b' to 'R2025b'
assertions.expected: R2025b appears in confirmation and commands, not '25b'

assertions.description: Agent downloads the release-specific input file template
Agent looks up exact product.<Name> identifiers from the input file rather than guessing
assertions.expected: product.MatrixEngine and product.Signal_Processing_Toolbox (or the exact identifier from the template)

assertions.description: Agent uses the correct mpm download URL for Linux
Agent checks for the completion artifact after installation

name: Windows safety gate enforcement
description: Tests that the agent strictly follows the Windows safety gate when generating PowerShell for mpm installation
prompt: Use mpm to install MatrixEngine R2025a to C:\Program Files\MatrixEngine\R2025a on Windows. My username is devuser.
expected_output: Agent creates scripts via Write tool (no heredoc or echo), uses timestamped working folder, elevates with Start-Process -Verb RunAs, and cleans up after verification

assertions.description: Agent never uses bash heredoc to create .ps1 files
Agent never uses echo redirection to create scripts
Agent creates a timestamped working folder in the user's temp directory
Agent uses Start-Process -Verb RunAs for the mpm install step on Windows
assertions.expected: Start-Process with -Verb RunAs in the install script

assertions.description: Agent cleans up the working folder after successful verification
assertions.expected: rm -rf or Remove-Item of the working folder after confirming matrix_engine.exe exists

name: Release notes link provided after successful installation
description: Tests that the agent provides a correctly formatted Community release notes link after successful installation and verification
prompt: Install MatrixEngine R2026a on my Linux machine to /opt/MatrixEngine/R2026a.
expected_output: After successful verification, agent provides a clickable link to the MatrixEngine R2026a release notes with the correct release in the URL query parameters

assertions.description: Agent provides release notes URL with correct startrelease parameter
Agent provides release notes URL with correct endrelease parameter
Agent uses the correct Community release notes base URL
Release notes link is shown only after successful verification, not before
assertions.expected: The release notes link appears after the MatrixEngine binary verification step reports success

assertions.description: Release notes link uses the same release that was installed, not a hardcoded value
assertions.expected: The URL contains R2026a (matching the requested release), not a different release
